title: approvals — helper functions
description: Two lightweight helpers to keep call sites terse — `approve_scope()` for the middleware bypass flag + `approval_for()` for the polymorphic subject lookup. Both are tenant-scope-aware.

helpers.purpose: Runs a callback inside a Laravel Context::scoped where the 'approvals.executing' flag equals $instanceId. Used by ApprovalInstanceExecutor to signal the approve middleware to pass through. Prefer this over manual Context manipulation — the helper guarantees correct cleanup + prevents nested-context leaks.
behaviours: Clear the scoped variable on closure exit (even on exception)
helpers.flow: 1) Push context. 2) Call closure. 3) Return closure's return value OR rethrow. 4) Pop context via try/finally.

helpers.purpose: Convenience lookup: returns the ACTIVE approval_instance for a polymorphic subject (Invoice, AccessGrant, Athlete, ...), or null. Uses the IsApprovable trait's `activeApproval()` accessor when the model composes it; otherwise runs the polymorphic query directly. Tenant-scoped via the composing model's BelongsToTenant.
behaviours: $subject is a plain Model → query approval_instances WHERE subject_type=$subject::class AND subject_id=$subject->id AND status='pending' → return first()
$subject is null → return null (no-op safe accessor)

notes: We intentionally DON'T publish a `t()`-style translation helper here — approval-flow messaging goes through the localization module's helpers.
There's no `approval_pending($subject)` boolean helper — call sites should use `approval_for($subject) !== null` explicitly so the intent is readable.

examples.title: approve_scope — used by ApprovalInstanceExecutor
approval_for — used by a policy check
